title: Prairie View A&M's run continues in First Four win

title: Why MGR’s election from Andipatti was challenged in the court?
content: The petitions did not question the polling or counting process. Instead, they raised a legal issue: whether MGR was constitutionally qualified to contest at all, in view of how and before whom he had made and subscribed the oath or affirmation mandated under Article 173 (a) of the Constitution.

title: Bihar Congress asks three truant MLAs to explain their absence during the RS polling
content: Their absence led to the defeat of the Mahagathbandhan candidate; the three MLAs alleged that the State Congress chief told them to skip voting as he had not been involved in choosing the alliance candidate

title: Free bus travel for persons with disabilities launched in Andhra Pradesh
content: The scheme allows persons with more than 40% disability to travel free of cost in several categories of APSRTC services; attendants of beneciaries to get 50% fare concession

title: Qatar expels Iranian diplomats after strikes on key energy facilities
content: Qatar expelled key Iranian military and security officials following missile strikes on its energy infrastructure. The Qatari Foreign Ministry declared the attachés and their staff persona non grata, citing repeated Iranian aggression and violations of sovereignty. This diplomatic fallout follows significant damage to the Ras Laffan Industrial City, a critical gas processing hub.

title: Water motor led to blaze? 3 kids among 9 of family killed in Delhi building fire
content: A heartbreaking incident unfolded in Palam, Delhi, when a raging fire consumed a mixed-use building, leading to the tragic loss of nine lives, including three innocent children. In a desperate bid to escape, three residents jumped from the upper floors, sustaining critical injuries. Preliminary investigations suggest that a short circuit may have sparked the inferno.

title: FBI adds ATM jackpotting gang leader to Most Wanted list; how the scams work
content: Anibal Alexander Canelon Aguirre, a leader of the transnational gang Tren de Aragua, has been added to the FBI's Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list. He is accused of orchestrating a large international conspiracy that deployed crews to the U.S. to steal millions from financial institutions through ATM jackpotting. The stolen funds allegedly supported the designated foreign terrorist organization.

title: Pakistan's Akhtar revives Mohammad Amir fixing memory with huge no-ball
content: The delivery was compared by fans to Mohammad Amir’s no-ball against England during the 2010 spot-fixing case at Lord’s, with Akhtar’s no-ball appearing big in comparison.

title: RIL, HDFC Securities and more: Top stocks to watch on March 19, 2026
content: Analysts maintain positive outlooks on Reliance Industries, M&M, and Max Healthcare, citing new deals, strong demand, and expansion plans. However, L&T faces near-term headwinds due to Middle East project execution concerns, leading to a reduced target price. Ask Automotive receives an 'add' rating with a focus on premiumization and electrification.

title: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang becomes first CEO to roll out token budgets for recruitment
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang proposed offering engineers annual "token budgets" as part of their compensation, potentially amounting to half their base salary. This move aims to boost engineer productivity and serve as a new recruiting tool in Silicon Valley. Huang believes this will amplify engineers' capabilities, making them significantly more effective.

title: UAE slams Iran’s ‘terrorist attack’ as missiles target Abu Dhabi's Habshan gas facility
content: Iran targeted the UAE's Habshan gas facility and Bab oil field. UAE air defence intercepted the threats. No casualties were reported. The UAE strongly condemned the attack, calling it a dangerous escalation. This incident highlights the growing vulnerability of critical energy infrastructure. Energy itself has become a battlefield in the intensifying Iran vs US-Israel war.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang updates on China after CFO flags no H200 revenue
content: Nvidia is set to resume sales of its H200 processors in China after receiving purchase orders and clearance from both US and Chinese authorities. This marks a significant shift following months of uncertainty and export restrictions. The company anticipates regaining a critical market for its global data center business.

title: Happy Ugadi 2026: 75+ wishes, images, quotes, cards, and GIFs
content: As Ugadi 2026 approaches, the article offers a guide to celebrating the New Year with heartfelt messages and aesthetic visuals. It delves into the philosophy of the Ugadi Pachadi, symbolizing life's diverse experiences, and provides tailored greetings for family, friends, and professional contacts. The piece also highlights modern trends in sharing wishes, encouraging personal touches over generic forwards.

title: Sam Altman sends 'thanks note' to engineers with the message that 'we plan to take your job'
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed gratitude to early software engineers, acknowledging their complex work. However, his comments sparked debate about AI's impact on coding jobs, with users interpreting his message as a prelude to displacement. This follows recent workforce reductions and AI investment announcements from companies like Block Inc. and Atlassian, signaling a broader trend of automation affecting white-collar roles.

title: MLB trade rumors: Red Sox may trade $90M star before Opening Day
content: Boston Red Sox are exploring trading Masataka Yoshida due to roster depth and financial pressures, exacerbated by his shoulder surgery. A potential deal sends Yoshida and a pitching prospect to Houston for infielder Isaac Paredes. This move offers Boston salary relief and a younger, versatile player, while Houston gains a contact hitter.

title: LeBron James receives heartbreaking news as his future NBA plans fall apart
content: LeBron James faces a major setback in his long-standing goal of owning an NBA team after Fenway Sports Group opted out of pursuing a Las Vegas expansion. With expansion fees reportedly reaching $8 billion and no primary financial backer, his plans now appear uncertain. Timing also remains an issue, as retirement would be required before any ownership bid becomes possible.

title: Dak Prescott’s ex Sarah Jane Ramos returns to spotlight after breakup
content: Sarah Jane Ramos resurfaced on social media following her high-profile split from Dak Prescott, offering a glimpse into life after a canceled wedding. The breakup, triggered by a heated argument during a Bahamas trip, ended months of private tension. While their Lake Como ceremony was called off with a formal note to guests, Ramos now shares quieter moments with her daughters. Her return signals a shift from public heartbreak to personal healing, highlighting how quickly circumstances can change.

title: Dubai’s green corridor with Oman goes live: Check details
content: Dubai and Oman have launched a new green corridor for sea-air cargo. This initiative promises faster and more efficient trade between Asia and the Middle East. Goods will move seamlessly between sea ports and air cargo facilities. This collaboration aims to reduce transit times and streamline customs. It positions the UAE as a key hub for next-generation global trade routes.

title: 4 burst crackers outside jail ‘to mark birthday of goon’ in Thane; 3 held
content: Khadakpada police in Kalyan have arrested three individuals for bursting firecrackers outside Adharwadi jail. The act was a celebration for the birthday of Satish alias Satya Bhai Kamble, a goon lodged in the prison. This created disturbance and raised security concerns, prompting swift police action and a public procession of the arrested accused.

title: Row over Nora Fatehi song reaches Parliament ; govt says it's banned
content: The hit song from 'KD: The Devil' has ignited a heated discussion in Parliament for its supposedly explicit lyrics. A Union minister has officially banned the track, stressing that the rights to free speech must consider cultural sensitivities. In response, the song's lyricist has issued an apology and is open to reworking the Hindi adaptation to address the concerns raised.

title: Middle East crisis: CBSE announces new evaluation rules for Gulf students
content: Amidst escalating Iran vs US-Israel war, CBSE has cancelled Class 10 board exams in Gulf countries, impacting over 50,000 students. A new evaluation scheme uses performance in conducted exams and internal assessments, with averages for missed papers. This unprecedented move prioritises safety, offering a second chance for improvement while preserving global result timelines.

title: Bahrain tells residents to take shelter as Middle East conflict escalates
content: Bahrain's siren system blares, urging residents to seek immediate shelter. This alert signals a serious escalation in the ongoing Iran vs US-Israel war. Emergency warnings and missile interceptions are becoming common across the Gulf. Civilians are now facing direct threats, transforming distant tensions into a lived reality. Preparedness is no longer optional as the region navigates this critical phase.

title: US Fed keeps interest rates unchanged; flags 'uncertainty' amid Iran war
content: Experts had said that fears of oil prices stoking inflation were unlikely to push the Federal Reserve toward raising interest rates at this week’s meeting. hile markets and economists had earlier anticipated that the next move would be a rate cut, that conviction has weakened since the United States and Israel launched airstrikes on Iran on February 28.

title: US spy chief says Iran not rebuilding nukes, undercutting Trump’s war rationale
content: US intelligence indicates Iran has not rebuilt nuclear enrichment capacity post-strikes, contradicting President Trump's war justification. Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard's testimony revealed no efforts to restore capability, despite Trump's claims of an imminent threat. This finding fuels Democratic pressure regarding the administration's rationale for the ongoing conflict.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s secretive gold party night triggers fan outrage
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce attended Jay-Z and Beyoncé’s exclusive Oscars afterparty, where Swift was seen dancing and mingling with A-list stars. The appearance sparked mixed fan reactions online. While some celebrated the star-studded moment, others questioned her presence, with comments like “I don’t understand why she went,” highlighting curiosity, criticism, and ongoing debate across social media.

title: Jack Hughes wants Olympic Golden-Goal Puck back, Hockey Hall of Fame denies request
content: New Jersey Devils star Jack Hughes is reportedly seeking the return of his Olympic golden-goal puck from the Hockey Hall of Fame for personal reasons. Hughes believes the historic keepsake should remain with him, but the Hall states the puck was officially donated and never his to own, citing IIHF protocol.

title: Rory McIlroy reveals Masters Menu story with Elk Sliders Irish champ
content: Rory McIlroy has revealed his Masters Champions Dinner menu, mixing luxury dishes with personal memories. From elk sliders to Irish champ and his mother’s recipe, the menu reflects his journey. The dinner will take place before the Masters at Augusta National. His choices celebrate his first Master's win and career Grand Slam achievement.

title: Zakat al Mal vs Zakat al Fitr: Key difference many Muslims still get wrong ahead of Eid
content: As Ramadan 2026 concludes, a key question arises: the distinction between Zakat al Mal and Zakat al Fitr. While both are mandatory Islamic charities, Zakat al Mal is an annual wealth-based obligation, whereas Zakat al Fitr is a fixed per-person charity due before Eid al Fitr prayer, ensuring inclusion for all.

title: 70% Kerala MLAs Face Criminal Cases, Over Half Are Crorepatis
content: Nearly 70 per cent of sitting MLAs in Kerala have declared criminal cases against themselves, while more than half are crorepatis, according to a report by the Association for Democratic Reforms.

title: US Fed Keeps Rates Unchanged: What It Means For Indian Stock Markets
content: US Federal Reserve's Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) kept federal funds rate unchanged at the range of 3.5 per cent-to-3.75 per cent.

title: 16 Mumbai Suburban Services To Get 15-Car Trains From March 19
content: The Western Railway will convert 16 existing 12-car suburban trains into 15-car services from March 19 in a bid to enhance carrying capacity and ease overcrowding on its network in Mumbai, officials said.

title: Iran War Highlights: Pentagon Seeks More Fund As Iran War Intensifies
content: US-Israel-Iran War Highlights: The war in the Middle East broke out when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

title: 'Iran No Where Close To Nukes': Joe Kent In 1st Interview After Exit
content: A day after his much-talked-about resignation, which he claimed was in protest of the Iran War, Joe Kent, US President Donald Trump's former counterterrorism chief, blamed Israel for pushing into the conflict.

title: World's Largest LNG Plant In Qatar Hit By Iranian Missile, Fire On Site
content: A Qatari complex housing the world's largest liquefied natural gas export plant suffered "extensive damage" amid a sharp escalation of attacks on energy facilities across the Persian Gulf.

title: Iran War LIVE: Iran Targets Gulf Energy Sites After Strike On Key Gas Field
content: US Israel Iran War News LIVE Updates: Iran has been striking its Persian Gulf neighbours' energy facilities since the war started on February 28, and has made the Strait of Hormuz shipping channel, nearly impassable.

title: Social Media Impacts Youth Wellbeing, Girls Most Affected: UN Report
content: This year's World Happiness Report highlighted the impact of social media as many countries impose or mull legislative restrictions on social media use for young people.

title: Inches From Ground, Chopper Loses Control. Horrifying Crash Seen In Video
content: A helicopter en route from Kathmandu to Nepal's Khotang district crashed on Wednesday while attempting a landing on a farm.

title: US Weighs Military Reinforcements As Iran War Enters Possible New Phase
content: President Donald Trump's administration is considering deploying thousands of US troops to reinforce its operation in the Middle East, as the US military prepares for possible next steps in its campaign against Iran,

title: FBI Hunts For Indian National In Mail, Wire Fraud Conspiracy
content: The Federal Bureau of Investigation of the US on Wednesday said it has launched a hunt for Indian national Kalpeshkumar Rasikbhai Patel for allegedly defrauding multiple victims between 2017 and 2021.

title: Qatar Expels Iranian Embassy Staff Over Gas Facility Attack
content: Qatar has declared the military and security attaches at the Iranian Embassy, along with their staff, "persona non grata" and has requested that they leave the country within 24 hours.

title: Pakistan Announces Eid Truce As Taliban Vows Kabul Attack Revenge
content: Pakistan announced on Wednesday a "temporary pause" in hostilities with Afghanistan to mark the end of Ramadan, after Kabul promised to avenge the deaths of hundreds killed in a strike on a Kabul drug treatment centre.

title: Qatar Says "Extensive Damage" Caused By Iran Attacks On Gas Facility
content: Qatar's state-run energy company said on Wednesday that fires set by Iranian strikes on its main gas facility had caused "extensive damage", after Tehran said it would retaliate in kind for an attack on its own energy infrastructure.

title: Air India To Operate 48 Middle East Flights Today, Dubai Services To Resume
content: Air India, Air India Express and IndiGo on Wednesday decided to resume services to Dubai while services to a few other cities in the region remain suspended.

title: China, Pak Developing Nuclear Missiles That Put US Within Range: Intel Chief
content: US Intelligence Chief Tulsi Gabbard, presenting the intelligence community's 2026 Annual Threat Assessment, said that Russia, China, North Korea, Iran and Pakistan are the most significant nuclear threats to the United States.

title: US Eases Venezuela Oil Sanctions To Allow Trade As Iran War Drives Up Prices
content: US companies will be allowed to do business with Venezuela's state-owned oil and gas company after the Treasury Department eased sanctions, with some limitations, on Wednesday.

title: 'Iran Wasn't Restarting Nuclear Program': US Intel Chief Contradicts Trump
content: US intelligence concluded Wednesday that Iran was not rebuilding nuclear enrichment capacities destroyed last year by the United States and Israel, contradicting a key justification by President Donald Trump for his ongoing war.

title: Pakistan, Afghanistan Announce 'Temporary Pause' In Fighting Due To Eid
content: Pakistan and Afghanistan on Wednesday announced a "temporary pause" in fighting in view of Eid-ul-Fitr and at the request of Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Qatar.

title: UP Man Kills 4-Year-Old Neighbour, Arrested After Encounter
content: A man accused of killing a 4-year-old girl in the Nandgram area here was arrested after an encounter in the early hours of Wednesday, officials said.
